Who was Julius Caesar?: Julius Caesar was a Roman general, statesman, and key figure in the rise of the Roman Empire.
Who was Napoleon Bonaparte?: Napoleon Bonaparte was a French military leader and emperor who conquered much of Europe.
Who was Christopher Columbus?: Christopher Columbus was an explorer who completed voyages across the Atlantic Ocean in 1492.
Who was George Washington?: George Washington was the first president of the United States.
Who was Abraham Lincoln?: Abraham Lincoln was the 16th president of the United States and led the country during the Civil War.
Who was Mahatma Gandhi?: Mahatma Gandhi was an Indian leader known for promoting nonviolent resistance.
Who was Martin Luther King Jr.?: Martin Luther King Jr. was an American civil rights leader known for his advocacy of racial equality.
Who was Nikola Tesla?: Nikola Tesla was an inventor and electrical engineer known for his contributions to alternating current power systems.
Who was Thomas Edison?: Thomas Edison was an inventor known for developing the practical electric light bulb and many other devices.
Who was Charles Darwin?: Charles Darwin was a naturalist who developed the theory of evolution by natural selection.
Who was Galileo Galilei?: Galileo Galilei was an astronomer, physicist, and mathematician who played a major role in the Scientific Revolution.
Who was Johannes Gutenberg?: Johannes Gutenberg invented the movable-type printing press in Europe.
When did the Roman Empire fall?: The Western Roman Empire is traditionally considered to have fallen in 476 AD.
When did the American Civil War begin?: The American Civil War began in 1861.
When did the American Civil War end?: The American Civil War ended in 1865.
What was the Renaissance?: The Renaissance was a cultural movement that began in Europe and emphasized art, science, and learning.
What was the Industrial Revolution?: The Industrial Revolution was a period of rapid industrialization and technological advancement.
What was the Cold War?: The Cold War was a period of political tension between the United States and the Soviet Union after World War II.
What was the Roman Empire?: The Roman Empire was one of the largest and most influential civilizations in ancient history.

When was Isaac Newton born?: Isaac Newton was born on January 4, 1643.
What did Isaac Newton do?: Isaac Newton developed the laws of motion, formulated universal gravitation, and made important contributions to mathematics and optics.
